title: Replace export ffmpeg argument blocklist with a structural allowlist
insight: A blocklist guarding user-supplied ffmpeg export args was bypassable (stream-specifier filters, scheme-less protocols, tee/preset/-/option file access) -> arbitrary file read/write + SSRF. Switched to an allowlist of encoder flags + safe filters.
nurby_evidence: shared/netpolicy.py covers webhook SSRF; ffmpeg arg surface (export/clip/stream URL) not audited for injection. SSRF was a deliberate skip per overnight-review memory.
action: Audit every user-influenced ffmpeg invocation (clip export, stream_url, mediamtx mux) for arg/protocol injection; adopt an allowlist.

title: Add ability to hide cameras from review UI
insight: Camera can be hidden from the review/alerts feed independently of being hidden from the live dashboard. Two separate visibility flags.
nurby_evidence: Camera wall view customization shipped (commit b870614) hides tiles from dashboard; no separate 'exclude from timeline/alerts feed' flag found.
action: Add per-camera 'exclude from review/timeline' flag, distinct from dashboard hide, that still records but drops the camera from the alerts/timeline feed and filters.

title: Restore runtime state on startup
insight: Runtime toggles (camera on/off, detect, recordings, snapshots, audio) persist across restarts via a .runtime_state.json overlaid on YAML provisioning, cleared on explicit config save / profile switch.
nurby_evidence: Nurby config is DB-driven (cameras + app_settings in Postgres), so runtime toggles already survive restarts by architecture. Frigate needed this only because YAML is its provisioning source.
action: None. Note the provisioning-vs-runtime precedence nuance if/when profiles are added.
